title: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)
concept: SFT trains the base model on a dataset of formatted (instruction, response) pairs, using standard cross-entropy loss to predict the response tokens.

exercises.prompt: Why can't we just use a raw base model as a helpful assistant right away?

options.text: They are already too polite and helpful.
They just predict the next word and often mimic the question instead of answering it.
They do not know any words at all.

exercises.explain: Base models are trained to predict the next word on raw web data, so they often continue prompts instead of answering them. SFT fixes this.

cards.title: The Wild Base Model
cards.body: When AI models are first built, they are like giant sponges that read the whole internet. They are great at completing sentences, but terrible at answering questions. If you ask them 'How do I bake a cake?', they might just ask you back 'How do I bake a cookie?'. How do we teach them to actually answer us? Enter Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)!

cards.title: What is SFT?
cards.body: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) is the process of taking a base model that already knows language, and training it further on a clean dataset of 'Prompt' and 'Response' pairs. Using a standard mathematical loss (cross-entropy), the model learns to look at a prompt and predict the exact words of the correct response.

cards.title: The Polite Student
cards.body: Imagine you have a student who is amazing at reading and knows every word in the dictionary, but doesn't know how to behave in school. Supervised Fine-Tuning is like giving this student a booklet of practice questions and answers. By reading these examples, the student learns exactly how to politely answer a teacher's questions instead of just shouting out random related words.

cards.title: Before and After SFT
cards.body: Before SFT, if you type: 'Write a poem about a cat.', the base model might just say: 'Write a poem about a dog. Write a poem about a bird.'\n\nAfter SFT, when you type: 'Write a poem about a cat.', the model responds with: 'Soft paws on the floor, chasing shadows out the door...' because it has learned to follow instructions!

cards.title: Does SFT Teach New Knowledge?
cards.body: No! SFT does not teach the model completely new facts about the world. Instead, it teaches the model how to use the knowledge it already has. It reformats the model's behavior so it acts like a helpful AI assistant instead of a random text predictor.

cards.title: Base vs. SFT Model
cards.body: Let's compare a Base Model (before SFT) with an Assistant Model (after SFT) when asked a question.

left.text: Input: 'How do you boil an egg?' -> Output: 'How do you boil a potato? How do you fry an egg?' (Just mimics internet text)

right.text: Input: 'How do you boil an egg?' -> Output: 'First, place the egg in boiling water for 6 minutes...' (Answers the question directly)

exercises.prompt: What training objective does Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) use?

options.text: Standard next-token prediction on formatted response text.
Randomly deleting words from the internet.
Comparing two different AI outputs and choosing the best one.

exercises.explain: SFT uses standard cross-entropy loss to train the model to predict the next token (word) in the target response.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Supervised Fine-Tuning requires training data that contains both prompts and correct responses.
exercises.explain: SFT uses formatted pairs of prompts and target responses to teach the model how to follow instructions.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing part of the training pair:
exercises.sentence: SFT trains a model on formatted pairs of instruction and [blank].
exercises.explain: During SFT training, we feed the model a prompt and teach it to generate the correct response tokens, word by word.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is formatted correctly for SFT training?

options.text: 'Trees are tall green plants that grow in forests.'
'Prompt: Describe a tree. Response: A tree is a woody perennial plant.'

exercises.explain: The prompt 'Describe a tree:' followed by the actual description is a perfect instruction-response pair for SFT training. The other options are either just general text or a single prompt with no answer.

exercises.prompt: How does Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) transform a wild, text-completing base model into a helpful assistant?

options.text: By letting it browse the internet in real time to find answers.
By giving it points whenever it guesses a random word correctly.
By showing it correct instruction-response pairs and training it to predict the response words.
By erasing its memory and starting its training completely from scratch.

exercises.explain: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) uses formatted examples (like Question/Answer pairs) and trains the model to predict the next word of the answer. It does not teach the model to browse the live internet or calculate rewards on the fly.
